w:r.w:t: BMAD Lite + Spec Kit Combined PRD

TBT Admin is a centralized administration platform designed to manage the Tamil Business Tribe ecosystem — a business growth community serving entrepreneurs, small business owners, freelancers, and aspiring entrepreneurs across India, with primary focus on Tamil Nadu.

The platform consists of two primary applications: (1) TBT Admin Web Application — the centralized control system for content management, member management, analytics, and business operations, and (2) TBT Mobile Application — the member-facing Flutter application for learning, task completion, community engagement, and business growth tracking.
The system is designed to support 200-300 concurrent users initially, with architectural provisions for scaling to 500,000+ users without major rewrites.
New Flutter App (separate from Habit Tracker)
Clean architecture, dedicated UX, no legacy debt
SSR, API routes, modern React, excellent DX
No separate Express server needed — reduces complexity
Role-based admin auth with enterprise features
Username + mobile + OTP fits mobile users
Realtime, RLS, Edge Functions, Storage — all-in-one
Cost-effective video delivery, DRM, adaptive streaming
Proven reliability on Android, already integrated
Indian-focused, affordable, official BSP, good API
Managed, auto-scaling, free tier for 300 users
Community access, 1 free course, limited podcast access
All free courses, all podcasts, daily tasks, basic analytics
All courses, live webinars, account manager, full analytics, badges
Everything + 1-on-1 mentoring, priority support, exclusive events, rewards catalog
Custom programs, dedicated account manager, white-label options
To build a business growth ecosystem that helps entrepreneurs learn, implement, measure, and improve their business performance through a centralized, data-driven, and community-powered platform.
20-35, starting first business, limited capital, motivated but confused
Learn fundamentals, start earning, validate ideas
Information overload, no roadmap, lack of accountability
Increase leads, sales, online presence, reduce agency dependency
Wasting money on agencies, no strategy, inconsistent execution
Team building, process optimization, automation, brand building
Growth plateau, poor systems, lack of accountability
Personal branding, client acquisition, lead generation
Build audience, launch programs, create digital assets
Platform Owner — full CRUD on everything
All modules, all data, all users
CRUD on content/members, no delete users, no settings
Virtual business manager for assigned members
Read/update assigned members, assign/review tasks, post in community
Subject matter expert — conducts sessions, creates content
Create courses/podcasts, manage live sessions, review assigned members
Moderate community posts, manage support tickets
Consume content, complete tasks, engage community, attend sessions
Stage 1: User Acquisition → Registration → Profile + Business Setup → Goal Selection
Stage 2: Onboarding → Welcome → Platform Walkthrough → Assessment → Dashboard
Stage 3: Program Enrollment → Browse → Subscribe → Enroll → Learning Roadmap
Stage 4: Learning Journey → Lessons → eBooks → Podcasts → Notes → Bookmarks
Stage 5: Implementation → Daily/Weekly Tasks → Upload Proof → Feedback → Improve
Stage 6: Accountability → Check-in → Attendance → Streaks → Progress Monitoring
Stage 7: Community → Posts → Comments → Likes → Wins → Questions → Challenges
Stage 8: Live Sessions → Register → Reminders → Attend → Resources → Recording
Stage 9: Gamification → Points → Streaks → Badges → Milestones → Leaderboard → Rewards
Stage 10: Business Growth Tracking → Leads → Marketing → KPIs → Recommendations
Stage 11: Support → Tickets → FAQ → Knowledge Base → Resolution
Stage 12: Achievement → Complete Program → Certificate → Share → Unlock Next
Stage 13: Retention → Advanced Programs → Challenges → Community → Events → Renewal

The system follows a modern API-first, modular architecture with clear separation between the admin web application, mobile application, and backend services. All data flows through Supabase PostgreSQL with Row Level Security policies enforcing access control at the database level.
Next.js API Routes + Supabase Edge Functions
Mobile App (Flutter) ←→ Supabase REST API + Realtime ←→ PostgreSQL Database
Admin App (Next.js) ←→ Next.js API Routes ←→ Supabase Client ←→ PostgreSQL Database
Supabase Edge Functions ←→ Firebase FCM (Push Notifications)
Supabase Edge Functions ←→ Wati API (WhatsApp Messages)

Your original stack included Express.js as a separate backend. This is unnecessary because Next.js API Routes provide the same functionality (REST endpoints, middleware, auth checks) without maintaining a separate server. This reduces deployment complexity, eliminates CORS issues, reduces costs (one deployment instead of two), and simplifies the codebase.
Tables: snake_case, plural (e.g., members, courses, task_submissions)
Status fields: status (TEXT with CHECK constraint)
Boolean fields: is_ prefix (e.g., is_active, is_milestone)
CHECK: super_admin, admin, account_manager, mentor, moderator
Array: seo, paid_ads, social_media, email, referral, offline
CHECK: free, starter, premium, vip, enterprise
CHECK: video, audio, pdf, ebook, text
quiz_questions (id, quiz_id, question_text, question_type, options JSONB, correct_answer)
quiz_attempts (id, member_id, quiz_id, score, passed, attempted_at)
lesson_progress (id, member_id, lesson_id, watch_percentage, completed, completed_at)
course_enrollments (id, member_id, course_id, enrolled_at, completed_at, progress_percentage)
programs (id, name, description, duration_days, incubation_days, status)
program_enrollments (id, member_id, program_id, enrolled_at, started_at, completed_at, status)
tasks (id, program_id, step_id, day_number, title, description, deliverables, content_url, base_points, proof_type, estimated_minutes, is_milestone, milestone_label, bonus_points, sort_order)
task_submissions (id, member_id, task_id, response_value, proof_url, proof_type, status, reviewed_by, reviewed_at, feedback)
podcasts (id, title, description, audio_url, thumbnail_url, category_id, access_type, duration_minutes, sort_order, is_published)
podcast_progress (id, member_id, podcast_id, listen_percentage, completed, last_position_seconds)
webinars (id, title, description, scheduled_at, duration_minutes, meeting_url, recording_url, status, max_attendees)
webinar_registrations (id, member_id, webinar_id, registered_at, attended, join_time, leave_time)
events (id, title, description, event_type, event_date, location, is_online, registration_url, max_attendees, status)
event_registrations (id, member_id, event_id, registered_at, attended, check_in_time)
community_posts (id, member_id, content, post_type, media_urls TEXT[], is_pinned, is_announcement, likes_count, comments_count, status)
community_comments (id, post_id, member_id, content, parent_comment_id, likes_count)
banners (id, title, image_url, link_url, target_audience, placement, priority, starts_at, expires_at, is_active)
support_tickets (id, member_id, category, subject, description, priority, status, assigned_to, resolved_at)
support_messages (id, ticket_id, sender_id, sender_type, message, attachments TEXT[])
notifications (id, member_id, title, body, type, data JSONB, is_read, sent_at)
notification_templates (id, event_type, title_template, body_template, channels TEXT[])
batches (id, name, description, program_id, starts_at, ends_at, is_active)
rewards (id, name, description, required_points, category, image_url, is_active)
reward_redemptions (id, member_id, reward_id, status, approved_by, approved_at)
badges (id, name, description, icon_url, criteria JSONB, badge_type)
milestones (id, program_id, name, day_number, criteria JSONB, bonus_points)
streaks (id, member_id, streak_type, current_count, longest_count, last_activity_date)
points_ledger (id, member_id, points, reason, reference_type, reference_id, created_at)
subscriptions (id, member_id, plan, razorpay_subscription_id, status, starts_at, ends_at, amount)
payments (id, member_id, subscription_id, razorpay_payment_id, amount, status, paid_at)
kyc_documents (id, member_id, document_type, document_url, status, verified_by, verified_at)
audit_logs (id, admin_id, action, table_name, record_id, old_data JSONB, new_data JSONB, created_at)
activity_logs (id, user_id, user_type, action, metadata JSONB, ip_address, created_at)
automation_rules (id, trigger_event, conditions JSONB, actions JSONB, is_active)
whatsapp_messages (id, member_id, template_name, message_body, status, sent_at)
before_after_assessments (id, member_id, program_id, assessment_type, data JSONB, video_url, created_at)
resources (id, title, resource_type, file_url, access_type, visibility JSONB, created_by)
faqs (id, category, question, answer, video_url, sort_order)
leaderboards (id, member_id, period, total_points, rank, calculated_at)
settings (id, key, value JSONB, category, updated_by, updated_at)
The dashboard provides a 360-degree real-time view of the entire TBT ecosystem.
Count of all registered members with trend indicator
Total revenue from subscriptions and courses
Date Range: Today, 7 days, 30 days, 90 days, Custom
Membership Plan: All, Free, Starter, Premium, VIP
Complete CRUD for internal team management with role-based access control.

All fields as specified in Section 4.2 admins table: profile photo, personal details (name, email, phone, DOB, blood group), role & organization (role dropdown, department, designation, reporting manager, employee ID), location (country → state → city dependent dropdowns, address), module-level permissions (checkbox matrix for all 12 modules × CRUD), authentication (username, status, password), notes & tags.
Columns: Name, Role, Department, Status, Last Login, Actions
The most complex module — manages the entire member journey from registration to retention.
All fields as specified in Section 4.2 members table across 7 sections: Basic Info, Business Info, Marketing Channels, Account Management, Program Assignment, KYC Documents, Action Buttons.
Learning: Courses enrolled, progress, completion, video watch hours
Tasks: Assigned, completed, pending, missed, approval rate
Attendance: Webinar attendance, event attendance, session duration
Gamification: Points, streaks, badges, milestones, leaderboard rank
Community: Posts, comments, likes, engagement score
App Usage: Daily/weekly/monthly active time, session duration, login frequency
Business Growth: Goals, revenue tracking, lead generation, KPIs
Churn Risk: Low/Medium/High (auto-calculated based on activity decline)
Advanced filters: Plan, Status, Batch, Account Manager, Sector, City, State, Health Score, Churn Risk
Bulk actions: Assign program, change plan, change account manager, send notification, export
Course → Module → Lesson (micro-video) with mandatory quiz-based progression.
Each lesson has a mandatory quiz. Flow: Watch Video → Attempt Quiz → Pass Quiz (≥70%) → Unlock Next Lesson. Members cannot skip lessons or modules. Progress is enforced sequentially.
Basic: Title, slug, description, thumbnail, trailer video, category, level, access type, price
Modules: Title, description, intro video, sort order
Lessons: Title, description, content type (video/audio/pdf/ebook/text), content URL, duration, quiz required, pass percentage
Quiz: Questions (MCQ, true/false, fill-blank), options, correct answer, explanation
Enrollment count, completion rate, average progress, drop-off analysis per lesson, watch time, quiz pass rates
Multiple programs supported: 90 Days Challenge, 21 Days Challenge, TBT Program, Textilepreneur, Medicopreneur, etc.

All fields as specified: Day number, step category (with drag-drop reordering), title, description, deliverables, content URL, base points, proof type, estimated time, milestone toggle, milestone label, bonus points.
Member submits → Account Manager/Mentor reviews → Approve/Reject with feedback → Points awarded → Streak updated → Leaderboard updated → Badge eligibility checked.
Task N+1 unlocks only after Task N is approved. No skipping.
Create webinar with LiveKit integration: title, description, scheduled time, duration, materials
Attendance tracking: join time, leave time, duration
Event creation: title, type (online/offline/hybrid), date, location, description
Banner CRUD: image, link, placement (home/course/webinar/event), target audience
Scheduling: start date, expiry date, priority order
Ticket management: create, assign, escalate, resolve
SLA tracking: first response time, resolution time
Podcast CRUD: title, audio file, thumbnail, category, series, access type
Player features: play, pause, resume, bookmark, speed control
Analytics: listening duration, completion rate, popular episodes, drop-off point
Post types: text, image, video, poll, announcement, win/achievement
Moderation: report, delete, pin, highlight, mute member
General: App name, branding, theme (light/dark), logo
Legal: Terms, privacy policy, refund policy (rich text editor)
Integrations: Firebase, Supabase, Wati, Razorpay, BunnyCDN, LiveKit
Security: Session management, API keys, audit logs
Gamification Config: Point values, streak rules, badge criteria, milestone definitions
Automation Rules: Inactivity triggers, nurturing sequences, reminder schedules
Every module follows RESTful API conventions. Base URL: /api/v1/
All list endpoints return: { data: [...], pagination: { page, per_page, total, total_pages } }
Mobile app consumes the same API with member-scoped access. RLS policies ensure members only see their own data. Additional mobile-specific endpoints:
Firebase Cloud Messaging via Supabase Edge Function
Wati API via Supabase Edge Function
Task: task_assigned, task_due_reminder, task_missed, task_approved, task_rejected
Webinar: registration_success, reminder_24h, reminder_1h, reminder_15min, started
Community: new_comment, new_like, mention, reply, announcement
Gamification: streak_maintained, streak_broken, badge_earned, milestone_achieved, rank_improved
Re-engagement: inactive_3d, inactive_7d, inactive_14d, incomplete_course, incomplete_task
Wati is recommended as the WhatsApp Business API provider. Cost: ~₹2,500/month for 1,000 conversations. Indian BSP, official Meta partner, good API documentation, template management built-in.
Streak at risk (no activity today by 8 PM)
Admin Auth: Clerk with role-based access (Super Admin, Admin, Account Manager, Mentor, Moderator)
Mobile Auth: Supabase Auth with phone + OTP
Database: Row Level Security (RLS) policies on all tables
API: JWT token validation on every request
File Access: Signed URLs with expiration for private files
Video: BunnyCDN token authentication for DRM-protected streams
Audit: Every create/update/delete logged to audit_logs table
Session: Token refresh with sliding expiration
Rate Limiting: API rate limiting per user (100 req/min)
Input Validation: Server-side validation on all endpoints
CORS: Whitelist only admin and mobile domains
While you requested building all 12 modules at once, here is the recommended implementation order within a single sprint cycle for maximum efficiency:
Database schema, Auth (Clerk + Supabase), Admin shell, Settings
Dashboard, Admin CRUD, Member CRUD with 360° view
Courses, Modules, Lessons, Quizzes, Progress tracking
Task CRUD, Programs, Steps, Submissions, Review workflow
Points, Streaks, Badges, Milestones, Leaderboard, Rewards
Flutter mobile app consuming all APIs
Estimated total: 24 weeks (6 months) for full platform with one senior developer.
Strict PRD adherence, change request process
BunnyCDN pay-per-GB, start with YouTube unlisted for MVP
Budget ₹2,500-5,000/month, use templates to minimize conversations
Start with Zoom links for MVP, migrate to LiveKit in Phase 2
Pagination, lazy loading, image caching, offline indicators
FCM via server-side (already solved with Edge Functions)
Use established patterns, shadcn/ui, Supabase client libraries

This document serves as the single source of truth for the TBT Admin Platform and Mobile Application ecosystem. All development should reference this PRD. Any changes must go through a formal change request process.
